summary: Conducted comprehensive user-needs analysis across 4 segments (Developers, PMs, Founders, Enterprises). Identified workflows, pain points, and willingness to pay for each. Key findings: 700K-1.5M non-developers blocked by technical barriers, validated $20-100/user/month pricing, confirmed 'Lovable for agents' positioning with evidence from user communities. Documented 5 competitive gaps and provided strategic recommendations for product development.
keyFindings: 4 distinct user segments with different workflows and willingness to pay
Developers spend 30-50% of time debugging, willing to pay $30-50/month for better tools
200K-500K PMs blocked on engineering for agent prototyping, want no-code solution
500K-1M founders can't build agent products without hiring engineers ($5K-20K cost)
Universal pain points: complexity, debugging difficulty, slow time-to-value, poor UX
5 competitive gaps: no visual/no-code platform, integrated debugging, sub-5-min value, non-developer access, all-in-one platform
Validated 'Lovable for agents' positioning - no competitor delivers this
Freemium pricing essential with aggressive free tier (500 runs/month)
Developer-first GTM then expand to PMs, founders, enterprises
nextSteps: PM writes ideation proposals (3 product concepts)
Await Researcher and GM ideation proposals
